layout: row-major float32 little-endian (numpy dtype <f4), one row per concept, row order = the `ids` list below (authored alphabetical, then synthetic by index)

shuffled.label: e4/shuffle/0
shuffled.note: row i of the shuffled table = kernel[perm[i]]; derangement (no fixed points) enforced

shuffled.label: e4/shuffle/1/retry2
shuffled.note: row i of the shuffled table = kernel[perm[i]]; derangement (no fixed points) enforced

shuffled.label: e4/shuffle/2/retry1
shuffled.note: row i of the shuffled table = kernel[perm[i]]; derangement (no fixed points) enforced

shuffled.label: e4/shuffle/3/retry3
shuffled.note: row i of the shuffled table = kernel[perm[i]]; derangement (no fixed points) enforced

shuffled.label: e4/shuffle/4/retry1
shuffled.note: row i of the shuffled table = kernel[perm[i]]; derangement (no fixed points) enforced

scalePolicy: kernel/shuffled rows are unit-norm here and are multiplied by frozenScale = initStd*sqrt(D) = 0.4525483399593905 at load time by the E4 runner so all arms' concept rows share the trainable-init norm scale (poc/e1 policy); randomFrozen rows are stored at their natural N(0, initStd^2) draw (norm-matched in distribution, not per-row)
